user_input:
Thank you. Better. 
You still have not added a visible outer halo. We need a much much thicker, more visible halo around the border. Please add a tweaks menu that lets me adjust the level of halo. Also, the map is inverted. As in, florida is normally in the southeast - on your map, it is in the southwest. Also, the map is upside down, so even if we fixed the inverting, it still means we're looking at florida at the top of the screen instead of at the bottom. That makes it less recognizable. Please adjust.

content:
Fixing map orientation and adding a thicker, adjustable US border halo. Inspecting how the map is projected and rendered.
Fixing map orientation (Z-axis flip so Florida sits southeast at the bottom) and adding a thicker, adjustable US border halo with a tweaks panel.
Fixing map orientation (Z-axis flip so Florida sits southeast at the bottom) and adding a thicker, adjustable US border halo with a tweaks panel.
Syncing the React export and verifying land mesh winding isn't inverted.
Syncing the React export and verifying land mesh winding isn't inverted.
Fixing asset paths in the React export copy.
Fixing asset paths in the React export copy.
Three fixes in `lightgbm-emissions-art-v2.html` (synced to the React export):

### Map orientation (north up, Florida southeast)
The projected Y axis was negated when mapping to 3D, which flipped the map vertically and made the US read backwards. Projection now uses:
- **South (Florida) → toward the camera** (bottom of screen)
- **North → away from camera** (top of screen)
- **East → right**, **West → left**

You should see a recognizable US silhouette with Florida at the bottom-right.

### Much thicker border halo
Coast outline now uses **5 stacked ribbon layers** (outer glow through bright core) instead of 3 thin ones. Default width is about **6×** the previous outer halo.

### Tweaks panel
A **Tweaks** button is in the top-right (hidden in `?embed=1` iframe mode). Open it and drag **US border halo** from **0.5× to 5×** — the coast rebuilds live as you adjust.

Default is **2.5×**. For an even bolder outline, try **3.5–4×**.

Refresh the preview and confirm Florida sits at the bottom-right with a visible glowing US border. If east/west still feels mirrored, say so and I can flip the X axis too.Three fixes in `lightgbm-emissions-art-v2.html` (synced to the React export):

### Map orientation (north up, Florida southeast)
The projected Y axis was negated when mapping to 3D, which flipped the map vertically and made the US read backwards. Projection now uses:
- **South (Florida) → toward the camera** (bottom of screen)
- **North → away from camera** (top of screen)
- **East → right**, **West → left**

You should see a recognizable US silhouette with Florida at the bottom-right.

### Much thicker border halo
Coast outline now uses **5 stacked ribbon layers** (outer glow through bright core) instead of 3 thin ones. Default width is about **6×** the previous outer halo.

### Tweaks panel
A **Tweaks** button is in the top-right (hidden in `?embed=1` iframe mode). Open it and drag **US border halo** from **0.5× to 5×** — the coast rebuilds live as you adjust.

Default is **2.5×**. For an even bolder outline, try **3.5–4×**.

Refresh the preview and confirm Florida sits at the bottom-right with a visible glowing US border. If east/west still feels mirrored, say so and I can flip the X axis too.
